scheme.name: PM Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME)

benefits.name: Credit-linked capital subsidy for individual micro food units
benefits.summary: Credit-linked capital subsidy to individual micro food processing units for setting up or upgrading, with a preference for One District One Product (ODOP) products.
benefits.calculation_logic: 35pct of eligible project cost as credit-linked capital subsidy, capped at 10 lakh per unit. Beneficiary brings minimum 10pct; balance is a bank loan. Repayment up to about 10 years with 6-24 month moratorium.

benefits.name: Seed capital to SHG members in food processing
benefits.summary: Seed capital provided to members of Self-Help Groups engaged in food processing, for working capital and purchase of small tools, routed through the SHG federation.
benefits.calculation_logic: Seed capital of 40,000 per SHG member engaged in food processing, subject to a maximum of 4 lakh per SHG federation. Provided as a grant to the federation, extended to members as a loan through the SHG.

benefits.name: Credit-linked grant for common infrastructure and branding/marketing
benefits.summary: Credit-linked grant to FPOs, SHGs, cooperatives and government agencies for common infrastructure (processing, storage, packaging, incubation), plus marketing and branding support for group beneficiaries.
benefits.calculation_logic: 35pct credit-linked grant for common infrastructure, subject to a maximum of 3 crore. Branding and marketing support to FPOs/SHGs/cooperatives/SPVs at 50pct of total expenditure (with DPR support up to 5 lakh from the State Nodal Agency).

metadata.notes: Original scheme period was 2020-21 to 2024-25 with a continuation into 2025-26; confirm current window. ODOP proposals preferred but non-ODOP also eligible. Union Bank of India is the nodal bank.

suggested_faqs.question: What is the main objective of PM Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME)?
suggested_faqs.answer: The main objective is to provide assistance and support under PM Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME) to eligible beneficiaries.

suggested_faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for PM Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME)?
suggested_faqs.answer: Businesses belonging to micro, women_led, sc_st_entrepreneur, rural_enterprise in food_processing may apply.
